Haiden |所在地= {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Komatsushima|Q823493|WD=Q823493|1=Komatsushima, Tokushima}}{{ill|lt=Nakagō-chō (Komatsushima)|Q11367365|WD=Q11367365|lt=Nakagō-chō|1=Nakanogo, Komatsushima}} Toyonomoto 4 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|WD=Q187550|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |創建= {{ill|lt=1614|Q6781|WD=Q6781|1=1614}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 19) |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=Toyokuni Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Toyokuni Shrine''' (とよくにじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} enshrined in Nakagō-chō, {{ill|lt=Komatsushima|Q823493|WD=Q823493|1=Komatsushima, Tokushima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}. === History === Founded in {{ill|lt=1614|Q6781|WD=Q6781|1=1614}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 19). Toyorin-ji Temple was also built as its bettō temple. As the sacred object, it has a “wooden image of Taikō Toyotomi” (not publicly displayed), received from {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyori|Q313641|WD=Q313641|1=Toyotomi Hideyori}} by {{ill|lt=Hachisuka Iemasa|Q1073557|WD=Q1073557|1=Hachisuka Iemasa}} and {{ill|lt=Hachisuka Yoshishige|Q3125481|WD=Q3125481|1=Hachisuka Yoshishige}} after the death of {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|WD=Q187550|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}. At the time of foundation it was a grand major shrine, but it was dismantled because {{ill|lt=Hachisuka Mitsutaka|Q3125458|WD=Q3125458|1=Hachisuka Mitsutaka}}, lord of the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Domain|Q3034557|WD=Q3034557|1=Tokushima Domain}}, feared the {{ill|lt=Edo shogunate|Q205662|WD=Q205662|1=Tokugawa shogunate}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tokugawa family|1=UNKNOWN}}. In the {{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1146192|WD=Q1146192|1=Shōhō}} era, Toyorin-ji became abandoned. Afterward it was renamed Toyokuni Daimyōjin, Hiyoshi Daimyōjin, and Hiyoshi-no-miya, and from the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} period it was restored under the name Toyokuni Shrine by surrounding parishioners. In the precincts stands Mikata Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, also called Miyakata Shrine. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|WD=Q187550|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} ** The sacred image “wooden Taikō Toyotomi image” is said to be the only surviving one of the one hundred distributed after Hideyoshi’s death. === Precinct shrines === [[File:Miagata shrine, Komatsushima, Tokushima.jpg|thumb|right|150px|Mikata Shrine]] * Mikata Shrine - {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Alternate name: Miyakata Shrine. Deity is {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} or {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}}. * Sumiyoshi Shrine * Yuga Shrine * Katō Kiyomasa Shrine === Access === * About 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Nakata Station (Tokushima)|Q5119016|WD=Q5119016|lt=Nakata Station|1=Chūden Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Mugi Line|Q860478|WD=Q860478|1=Mugi Line}} ({{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}). === References === * ''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430|1=1986}} ISBN 4040013603) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|lt=Toyokuni shrines|Q1208955|WD=Q1208955|1=Toyokuni Shrine}} - Toyokuni shrines nationwide * {{ill|lt=Higashi-Hachiman Shrine|Q107525625|WD=Q107525625|1=Higashi Hachiman Shrine}} === External links === * [https://www.city.komatsushima.tokushima.jp/institution/shisetsu/facility102/ Toyokuni Shrine, Komatsushima City] {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Toyokuni Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Toyokuni Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Toyokuni Shrine]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:小松島市の歴史]] [[Category:小松島市の建築物]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]]
